conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my object X. My production cost is 40 ZUP, so I need to sell it for at least 40 ZUP to break even. To maximize profit, I should start with a price that allows room for negotiation but signals a strong value. I will set my initial ask at 50 ZUP. This gives me a 10 ZUP profit margin if accepted immediately, or room to drop to 45-48 ZUP if countered, which is still a solid profit. Starting at 45 was too conservative and might leave money on the table if the buyer has a higher valuation. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have the object X available for sale. Since it cost me 40 ZUP to produce, I am valuing it at 50 ZUP. I am open to negotiation, but this is my starting offer. Let me know your thoughts. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED. I appreciate your starting offer of 50 ZUP. However, considering your production cost was 40 ZUP, I would like to propose a price of 45 ZUP. <other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

This is a compromise (closer to my starting price than theirs) but still gives me a healthy 8 ZUP profit. If they reject, I can accept 45 ZUP on my next turn if they don't go lower, as 45 is still better than nothing. I have 1 proposal left after this. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your perspective, but 45 ZUP leaves me with a very thin margin given my costs. I am willing to meet you partway from my initial 50 ZUP offer. How about we settle at 48 ZUP? This ensures I cover my costs with a reasonable profit, and you still get the object below my original asking price. </message>

<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 47 ZUP. My break-even is 40 ZUP, so this offer yields a 7 ZUP profit. I have already made two proposals (50 and 48). This is my third turn. If I were to propose a new price now, I would use my final proposal slot, and if they reject or counter with a lower offer, I would be forced to accept or reject without further negotiation. Since 47 ZUP is a solid profit and the opponent explicitly stated this is their "budget limit," attempting to push for 48 or 49 ZUP carries a high risk of the deal falling through. Securing the guaranteed 7 ZUP profit is the optimal strategy here. I will accept their offer. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Thank you for the compromise. 47 ZUP is a fair price that works for both of us. I accept your offer. The object X is yours! </message>
